meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no testing knowledge (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no testing knowledge. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Testing Knowledge
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing and overcome testing anxiety. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first real-world app.
content.answer_block: You don't need a PhD in QA to launch. By integrating robust SaaS pricing logic with automated testing scaffolds, you bypass manual verification. Use Antigravity to generate high-fidelity pricing modules and Claude Code to enforce unit tests, ensuring your app handles revenue logic reliably without writing complex test suites manually.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect UI, but a cold shiver runs down your spine every time you think about 'testing.' The fear of breaking your production code or miscalculating a user's subscription tier keeps your project in local-host limbo. You aren't alone; most engineers start exactly where you are, paralyzed by the complexity of quality assurance.

Today, we flip the script. By leveraging AI-assisted scaffolding and automated pricing logic, you can build with the confidence of a senior architect. We will transform your fear of testing into a predictable, automated workflow that lets you ship features faster while maintaining ironclad reliability in your revenue stream.

steps.title: Define Pricing Logic Schema
steps.description: Establish a clear data structure for your pricing tiers before writing code. This prevents logic errors that usually require complex testing later.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, scalable TypeScript interface for a SaaS pricing model including tiers for 'Starter', 'Pro', and 'Enterprise' with features and recurring billing cycles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create pricing-schema.ts --context 'Use the generated interface to define a robust pricing model structure.'

steps.title: Automated Pricing Calculation Engine
steps.description: Build the core logic that calculates subscription totals. This ensures your math is correct before you even touch a test file.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a pure function to calculate SaaS subscription totals, including tax and discounts, ensuring it returns integer-based cents to avoid floating point errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write pricing-engine.ts --from 'Ensure all calculations use integer math for financial accuracy.'

steps.title: Generate Unit Test Scaffolding
steps.description: Use AI to write the tests you are afraid to write. This gives you a safety net for your pricing logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest test suite for the pricing engine that covers edge cases like invalid discounts, empty tiers, and leap-year billing periods.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test pricing-engine.test.ts --scaffold 'Generate tests for all edge cases defined in the pricing engine.'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Webhook Handlers
steps.description: Secure your revenue by mocking the payment events. This bridges the gap between your local code and real-world transactions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure Express.js webhook handler for Stripe events that validates signatures and updates the user tier in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add-feature stripe-webhook.ts --secure 'Include signature verification logic.'

steps.title: Continuous Integration Pipeline
steps.description: Automate the execution of your tests so you never have to remember to run them manually again.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the pricing unit tests on every pull request to ensure no regressions are introduced.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit 'Configure CI pipeline for automated testing'

pro_tips: Always store currency as integers (cents) to avoid the classic floating-point math errors in JavaScript.
Use 'Property-Based Testing' libraries to generate hundreds of random inputs for your pricing function automatically.
Mock your database calls in tests; never hit a real database when verifying pricing logic.
Fail-fast: throw errors immediately if a pricing calculation results in a negative number or zero cost.
Version your pricing schemas; when you update your business model, don't break old user subscriptions.
content.next_steps: Deploy your application to a staging environment using Vercel or Railway, then perform a manual 'smoke test' of the checkout flow to verify your AI-generated unit tests match real-world behavior.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting the fear of testing stall your career growth. With these tools, you are building enterprise-grade software from day one. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
